theory.canonical_statement: A quantum information unit (qunit) in the Trawinistic framework is defined as a minimal Hilbert space of dimension ddd associated with a quantum subsystem; its implementation may be qubit (d=2d=2d=2) or higher-dimensional, with states encoded in curvature-protected degrees of freedom.

external_sources.title: Quantum Information Units
external_sources.canonical_claim: A quantum information unit (qunit) in the Trawinistic framework is defined as a minimal Hilbert space of dimension ddd associated with a quantum subsystem; its implementation may be qubit (d=2d=2d=2) or higher-dimensional, with states encoded in curvature-protected degrees of freedom.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Qudits and High‑Dimensional Quantum Computing) supports the definition (qudit/qunit) by showing that The article states that a qudit is a quantum version of d‑ary digits whose state can be described by a vector in a d‑dimensional Hilbert space.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: The article states that a qudit is a quantum version of d‑ary digits whose state can be described by a vector in a d‑dimensional Hilbert space. The space is spanned by a set of orthonormal basis vectors, and qudits can replace qubits as basic computational elements. This supports defining quantum information units (qunits) as d‑dimensional Hilbert spaces.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.
